title: Best VPN for Portugal 2026: Top Picks for Privacy, Streaming, and Travel
description: The best VPNs for Portugal in 2026: NordVPN, ExpressVPN, Surfshark, and Mullvad compared. Covers streaming, public Wi-Fi security, and accessing Portuguese content abroad.
content:
Portugal has some of the best internet infrastructure in Southern Europe and genuine internet freedom. But whether you are visiting Lisbon, living in the Algarve, or working remotely from Porto, there are real reasons to use a VPN -- from securing public Wi-Fi to accessing streaming content that is geo-blocked in Portugal.

Why Use a VPN in Portugal

Portugal does not block or censor the internet, so privacy and security are the main drivers here. Public Wi-Fi across hotels, cafes, and airports in major tourist areas is unsecured. A VPN encrypts all traffic on these networks and prevents eavesdropping or credential interception -- relevant for anyone doing banking or work communication on the road.

Streaming is the second most common use case. Portugal's domestic streaming services (NOS, MEO, SIC, RTP Play) are accessible within the country, but many expatriates and travelers want access to content libraries from the UK, Germany, or the US that are geo-blocked in Portugal. A VPN with servers in those countries bypasses the geo-restriction.

Best VPNs for Portugal in 2026

NordVPN

NordVPN operates servers in Lisbon and is consistently the top performer in independent speed tests across Europe. The NordLynx protocol (WireGuard-based) gives it an edge in raw throughput. The interface is clean on Windows, Mac, iOS, and Android. A two-year subscription drops the cost to around €3-4/month. NordVPN's threat protection feature blocks ads and known malicious domains at the VPN level, which is useful on public Portuguese Wi-Fi networks.

ExpressVPN

ExpressVPN's Lightway protocol is competitive with NordLynx and is particularly consistent on mobile connections -- relevant for travelers moving between hotel Wi-Fi and mobile data. ExpressVPN has servers in Portugal and routes efficiently to UK and US servers for streaming. It is more expensive than NordVPN at full price, but its reliability record and customer support are strong. Best for users who prioritize consistency over cost.

Surfshark

Surfshark is the budget option with no device limit -- one subscription covers every device you own. Speed is competitive in Europe, and Surfshark has a solid Portuguese server presence. The CleanWeb feature blocks ads and trackers. Best for families or users with many devices who do not want to pay per-device.

Mullvad

Mullvad is the privacy-first option. It accepts anonymous payment (cash, cryptocurrency), keeps no logs, and has a simple flat fee (about €5/month, no long-term commitments). Speed is good but not class-leading. Mullvad does not market itself for streaming and does not guarantee access to geo-locked content. Best for users whose primary concern is surveillance and ISP tracking rather than streaming.

Using a VPN for Portuguese Streaming Abroad

Portuguese expatriates who want to access RTP Play, SIC Online, or NOS streaming outside Portugal need a VPN with a Lisbon or Oporto server. NordVPN and ExpressVPN both work reliably for this use case. Connect to a Portuguese server before opening the streaming app, and the service will treat you as a domestic user. Note that some services periodically block VPN IP ranges -- if you encounter a block, switch to a different server within the same country.

What to Look for When Choosing a VPN for Portugal

Key criteria for Portugal: (1) Server presence in Lisbon for low-latency local connections. (2) Speed with WireGuard or an equivalent modern protocol. (3) Reliable unblocking of your target streaming services -- check current user reports, as streaming VPN access changes frequently. (4) A strict no-logs policy, audited by a third party. (5) Kill switch to prevent traffic leaking on dropped connections -- especially important on mobile and public Wi-Fi.
